methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $127M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cambium, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Simon Waddington and Stephan Herrera, has raised $127M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—alongside a broader universe of nine private and one public comparable in robotics and manufacturing.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $127M raised proxy, yielding $241M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies produces $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B, reflecting the distribution of same-sector valuations at Series B and early Series C. 

The triangulated analysis produces a low bound of $205M, a median of $933M, and a high bound of $1.9B. The wide range reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which treats raised capital as a conservative floor, and peer-median valuation, which anchors to observed market prices for comparable-stage competitors. Key uncertainties include Cambium's relative unit economics, technology differentiation versus the peer set, and burn trajectory relative to capital raised.
